what_it_does: Stores two kept grown design specs, each with a brief, root line, three-page plan, face identifiers, a derived note, and a kept timestamp.
observations: Top-level object contains a single grown array with two entries.
Each entry has an id, spec, and kept timestamp.
Both specs describe teaser-tier first-look pitch arcs with n set to 16.
Each plan has three pages with roles, archetype, title, and dials.
The first plan has page titles filled in; the second plan has empty page titles.
Both specs use harbour, tide, berth, and record language.
standouts: The file records generated design plans rather than rendered output.
The face references split sequence, voice, layouts, and grammar into separate ids.
The same three-page role structure appears in both entries.
conventions: Entries follow a repeated id/spec/kept structure.
Plan pages are numbered sequentially from 1 to 3.
Dials consistently include warmth, register, and density.
The second entry breaks the page-title convention by leaving titles empty.
concerns: Empty title fields in the second plan may make the plan less inspectable.
There is no explicit schema or version field for the grown entry format.
notes: This file is a small ledger of kept grown narrative-design plans with brief metadata and face references.
questions: Are empty page titles valid drafts, placeholders, or missing generated content?
What does n count in the brief?
What are the referenced face ids resolved against?
purpose_vs_actual: The brief-level purposes match the stored teaser pitch plans; there is no separate file-level purpose stated.
intention_signals: It seems to preserve generated pitch-plan substrates so they can be inspected, reused, or connected to referenced sequence, voice, layout, and grammar faces.
novelty: The file treats narrative design pieces as kept ledger entries composed from named face dimensions and page-level role plans.
contribution: Provides stored grown specs that contribute concrete brief data, root statements, three-page narrative structure, presentation dials, and compositional face references for a fused system to retain as design substrates.
summary_for_embedding: A JSON ledger containing two kept grown teaser pitch design specs with briefs, root lines, three-page plans, dials, face ids, and timestamps.
intention_for_embedding: This file was for preserving generated narrative-design plan artifacts and their compositional references for later interpretation or reuse.
